nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
# 🗂️ Bulk File Upload to Google Drive with Folder Management

## Overview
This workflow processes a form submission that accepts:
- Multiple file uploads (any format)
- Target folder name input

The workflow automatically:
- Checks if the specified folder exists in Google Drive
- Creates the folder if it doesn't exist
- Uploads all files to the correct folder
- Maintains original file names and structure

Perfect for batch uploading files while keeping your Drive organized!

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## 🔄 Decision Point: Folder Check
The workflow splits into two paths based on folder existence:
- ✅ TRUE: Use existing folder path
- 🆕 FALSE: Create new folder path

## 🗂️ Existing Folder Path (Upper)
1. Prepare Files for Upload: Splits files for individual processing
2. Upload Files: Uploads to existing folder maintaining structure

## 📁 New Folder Path (Lower)
1. Create Folder: Generates new folder in Drive
2. Prepare Files for New Folder: Splits files for individual processing
3. Upload to New Folder: Uploads to newly created folder

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## ⚙️ File Processing Notes
- All binary files are split individually for proper upload handling
- Original file names and structure are preserved
- Both paths ensure identical file organization

also see {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## 🔍 Search Query Pattern

The following search pattern looks for a folder with the specified name in a particular parent folder:
Make sure to replace <folderId>

```javascript
mimeType='application/vnd.google-apps.folder' and name = '{{ $json.folderName }}' and '<folderId>' in parents
```

**Important**: Marl Always Output Data so you can check also if nothing found.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.formTitle: Batch File Upload to Google Drive

parameters.content:
# 🗂️ Bulk File Upload to Google Drive with Folder Management

## Overview
This workflow processes a form submission that accepts:
- Multiple file uploads (any format)
- Target folder name input

The workflow automatically:
- Checks if the specified folder exists in Google Drive
- Creates the folder if it doesn't exist
- Uploads all files to the correct folder
- Maintains original file names and structure

Perfect for batch uploading files while keeping your Drive organized!

## 🔄 Decision Point: Folder Check
The workflow splits into two paths based on folder existence:
- ✅ TRUE: Use existing folder path
- 🆕 FALSE: Create new folder path

## 🗂️ Existing Folder Path (Upper)
1. Prepare Files for Upload: Splits files for individual processing
2. Upload Files: Uploads to existing folder maintaining structure

## 📁 New Folder Path (Lower)
1. Create Folder: Generates new folder in Drive
2. Prepare Files for New Folder: Splits files for individual processing
3. Upload to New Folder: Uploads to newly created folder

## ⚙️ File Processing Notes
- All binary files are split individually for proper upload handling
- Original file names and structure are preserved
- Both paths ensure identical file organization

also see {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## 🔍 Search Query Pattern

The following search pattern looks for a folder with the specified name in a particular parent folder:
Make sure to replace <folderId>

```javascript
mimeType='application/vnd.google-apps.folder' and name = '{{ $json.folderName }}' and '<folderId>' in parents
```

**Important**: Marl Always Output Data so you can check also if nothing found.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Drive  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "On form submission".
Open the "Search specific folder" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Folder found ?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Folder" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Upload Files" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Upload to New Folder" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Get Folder Name".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
